prompt:
[capsule v1 debugging]
anchors: "<" | "401"
req: backcompat; security; minimal_diff; one_regression_test
code: export async function authMiddleware(req,res,next){const token=getBearerToken(req);if(!token)return res.status(401).json({error:'missing_token'});const payload=verifyJwt(token);const nowMs=Date.now();const expMs=payload.exp * 1000;if(expMs<nowMs){return res.status(401).json({error:'expired_token'});}req.userId=payload.sub;return next();}
issue: tokens that expire exactly at the boundary sometimes trigger a 401 loop during refresh because another service allows a 30-second grace window for clock skew. Suggest the minimal fix and the regression test in the shortest correct form.
deliver: min_fix; reg_test

[capsule v1 review]
anchors: "x-user-id" | "401"
diff: - const userId=session.userId;+ const userId=req.headers['x-user-id'] || session.userId;if(!userId)return res.status(401).end();
context: this handler is behind the public API gateway. Keep the report concise but include risk and verification.
deliver: concise_risk; mitigation; verification

[capsule v1 refactor]
anchors: "async" | "await" | "next(err)"
target: loadUser
constraint: same_validation_order
error_path: "next(err)"
req: async_await; minimal behavioral change. Keep the same validation order and keep one place that maps database errors to `next(err)`.
code: function loadUser(id,next){if(!id)return next(new Error('missing_id'));db.findUser(id,function(err,user){if(err)return next(err);if(!user)return next(new Error('not_found'));audit.log(user.id,function(logErr){if(logErr)return next(logErr);cache.set(user.id,user,function(cacheErr){if(cacheErr)return next(cacheErr);next(null,user);});});});}
deliver: migration_plan_or_patch_sketch
